title: FDA Laser Incident Reporting Knowledge Graph
description: Procedural knowledge graph for FDA laser incident reporting requirements

nodes.label: Incident Reporting Process
nodes.description: Process for reporting laser-related adverse events to FDA

nodes.label: MEDWATCH
nodes.description: FDA's safety information and adverse event reporting program

nodes.label: Form FDA 3500
nodes.description: Voluntary adverse event reporting form

nodes.label: Form FDA 3500A
nodes.description: Mandatory adverse event reporting form for manufacturers

nodes.label: Manufacturer
nodes.description: Entity with mandatory reporting obligations

nodes.label: Importer
nodes.description: Entity importing laser products with reporting obligations

nodes.label: Healthcare Professional
nodes.description: Can submit voluntary reports

nodes.label: Consumer
nodes.description: Can submit voluntary reports

nodes.label: Death
nodes.description: Fatality associated with laser product use - always reportable

nodes.label: Serious Injury
nodes.description: Life-threatening, permanent impairment, or requiring medical intervention

nodes.label: Malfunction
nodes.description: Product failure that could cause harm if recurrent

nodes.label: Ocular Injury
nodes.description: Eye injury from laser exposure exceeding MPE

nodes.label: Skin Injury
nodes.description: Serious burns or tissue damage from laser exposure

nodes.label: 5-Day Report
nodes.description: Expedited timeline for death/serious injury requiring immediate action

nodes.label: 30-Day Report
nodes.description: Standard timeline for most reportable events

nodes.label: Internal Investigation
nodes.description: Manufacturer investigation of reported event

nodes.label: Root Cause Analysis
nodes.description: Determination of underlying cause of event

nodes.label: Corrective Action
nodes.description: Implementation of fixes to prevent recurrence

nodes.label: Supplemental Report
nodes.description: Additional report when new information becomes available

nodes.label: Maximum Permissible Exposure
nodes.description: Safety threshold for laser exposure

nodes.label: 10-Year Retention
nodes.description: Investigation records must be kept for 10 years or 2 years beyond device life

nodes.label: Online Submission
nodes.description: Submit via www.accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/medwatch/

nodes.label: Fax Submission
nodes.description: Submit to 1-800-FDA-0178

nodes.label: Mail Submission
nodes.description: Send to FDA MEDWATCH, 5600 Fishers Lane, Rockville, MD 20852-9787

nodes.label: Laser Wavelength
nodes.description: Required parameter in incident report

nodes.label: Output Power/Energy
nodes.description: Required parameter in incident report

nodes.label: Exposure Duration
nodes.description: Required parameter in incident report

nodes.label: NOHD
nodes.description: Nominal Ocular Hazard Distance - required parameter

nodes.label: Eye Protection
nodes.description: Documentation of protective measures in place

nodes.label: Medical Evaluation Results
nodes.description: Required documentation of injury assessment

nodes.label: Product Recall
nodes.description: Corrective action that requires supplemental reporting

process_flows.name: Mandatory Incident Reporting
steps: Submit Form FDA 3500A within required timeframe (5 or 30 days)
Maintain investigation records for required period

process_flows.name: Voluntary Incident Reporting
steps: Observe adverse event or product problem
Submit via MEDWATCH (online, fax, or mail)

process_flows.name: Supplemental Reporting
steps: Determine if supplemental report is required

decision_trees.name: Is the Event Reportable?
decision_trees.root: Did an event occur involving a laser product?

branches.result: REPORTABLE - Submit within 30 days (5 days if imminent hazard)

branches.condition: Serious injury (life-threatening, permanent impairment, medical intervention required)
branches.result: REPORTABLE - Submit within 30 days (5 days if immediate action needed)

branches.condition: Would likely cause death or serious injury if recurrent
Would not likely cause harm if recurrent
Exposure exceeded MPE and caused injury
Exposure did not exceed MPE or no injury
No injury, no malfunction, no death
